database_description: Payment transaction ledger for India-based recipients receiving international payments. Senders pay in GBP, EUR, or USD. The platform converts the full amount to INR, deducts a fee in INR, and pays out the remainder in INR. Each payment creates exactly 3 rows (legs) linked by transaction_group_id.

default_filters.always_filter_by_user_id: Every query must include WHERE user_id = <user_id>.
default_filters.for_received_amount: Use transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' and posting_amount (INR) to get net amount received.
default_filters.for_sender_currency_analysis: Use transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYMENT' and transaction_currency + transaction_amount for what the sender paid.
default_filters.for_fees: Use transaction_type = 'PAYOUT_FEE' and posting_amount (INR) for fees paid.
default_filters.never_mix_legs: Never SUM posting_amount across all three legs — this triples the real value. Always filter by a specific transaction_type.
default_filters.default_time_window: If no time range specified, default to last 90 days using created_at.

fx_context.description: The platform converts sender currency to INR at time of transaction. Approximate rates for context only — do not use for calculations, use posting_amount instead.
fx_context.fee: 1.5% of the gross INR amount, deducted before payout

tables.name: transactions
tables.description: Each real-world payment creates 3 rows all sharing the same transaction_group_id. INVOICED_PAYMENT = gross in sender currency. INVOICED_PAYOUT = net deposited to user in INR. PAYOUT_FEE = platform fee in INR.

columns.name: transaction_group_id
columns.description: Links all 3 legs of one payment. Use to JOIN or identify a complete transaction.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: The India-based account holder receiving the payment. Always filter by this.

columns.name: full_name
columns.description: Full name of the account holder (the recipient/user, NOT the sender).

columns.name: transaction_type
columns.description: Which leg of the payment this row represents.

values.INVOICED_PAYMENT: Gross amount received from the sender in their currency. posting_currency = sender currency.
values.INVOICED_PAYOUT: Net INR amount deposited to the user after fee deduction. posting_currency = INR. Use this for 'how much did I receive' questions.
values.PAYOUT_FEE: Platform fee charged in INR. posting_currency = INR. Use this for 'how much did I pay in fees' questions.

columns.name: transaction_amount
columns.description: Amount in the original sender currency (transaction_currency). Same value across all 3 legs of a group. Do NOT use this for INR totals.

columns.name: transaction_currency
columns.description: ISO 4217 code of the sender's currency. Use this to answer 'which currency do I receive most in'. Values: GBP, EUR, USD.

columns.name: posting_amount
columns.description: The settled amount in posting_currency. For INVOICED_PAYMENT this is in sender currency. For INVOICED_PAYOUT and PAYOUT_FEE this is in INR.

columns.name: posting_currency
columns.description: Currency of posting_amount. For INVOICED_PAYMENT = sender currency (GBP/EUR/USD). For INVOICED_PAYOUT and PAYOUT_FEE = INR always.

columns.name: sender_name
columns.description: Name of the person or company who sent the payment from abroad. Use for 'who pays me most' questions. One sender can have many transactions.

columns.name: customer_reference
columns.description: Internal customer reference code. Not the same as user_id.

columns.name: transaction_source
columns.description: How the transaction was initiated. Currently always PAYMENT_REQUEST.

columns.name: transaction_status
columns.description: Current status of the transaction. Currently always COMPLETED.

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: When the transaction was created. Use DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) to group by month. Default to last 90 days if no range specified.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: When the transaction record was last updated. Usually a few minutes after created_at.

common_questions.question: Who pays me the most by total INR value?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT sender_name, SUM(posting_amount) AS total_inr FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' GROUP BY sender_name ORDER BY total_inr DESC LIMIT 10

common_questions.question: Who sends me the highest number of transactions?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT sender_name, COUNT(*) AS num_transactions FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' GROUP BY sender_name ORDER BY num_transactions DESC LIMIT 10

common_questions.question: Which currency do I receive most payments in?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT transaction_currency, COUNT(*) AS count, SUM(transaction_amount) AS total_sender_amount FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYMENT' GROUP BY transaction_currency ORDER BY count DESC

common_questions.question: How much have I received in total in INR?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT SUM(posting_amount) AS total_inr FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT'

common_questions.question: How much did I receive this month in INR?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT SUM(posting_amount) AS total_inr FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' AND DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)

common_questions.question: How much did I pay in fees?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT SUM(posting_amount) AS total_fees_inr FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'PAYOUT_FEE'

common_questions.question: Show my monthly received amounts in INR
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month, SUM(posting_amount) AS total_inr FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month DESC

common_questions.question: What is the highest single payment I received?
common_questions.sql_pattern: SELECT sender_name, transaction_amount, transaction_currency, posting_amount, created_at FROM transactions WHERE user_id = ? AND transaction_type = 'INVOICED_PAYOUT' ORDER BY posting_amount DESC LIMIT 1
